cswsh.description: Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking — no Origin validation

probes.description: WebSocket handshake with Origin: https://evil.com — check if 101 returned

auth_bypass.description: WebSocket authentication bypass — missing auth on upgrade

probes.description: WebSocket upgrade without cookies/auth — check if connection accepted

injection.description: WebSocket message injection — SQLi/XSS in WS messages

message_smuggling.description: WebSocket-to-HTTP request tunneling — send HTTP requests inside WS frames to reach internal backends

probes.description: HTTP request inside WebSocket frame — test if backend processes raw HTTP sent via WS

proto_pollution.description: Prototype pollution via WebSocket JSON messages in Node.js applications

probes.description: Prototype pollution via WS JSON message — check if canary appears in subsequent responses

ws_auth_pre_handshake_send.description: WebSocket authentication race — server registers connection before validating auth, allowing first-message attack window. Common in custom WS servers, less common in Apollo / standard frameworks.

probes.description: Send authenticated message immediately after handshake without waiting for ACK. If processed before auth check completes, partial response leaks.

ws_session_replay.description: Session-ID replay on WebSocket upgrade — server trusts session cookie / token from a previous session, allowing attacker who captured a session ID to upgrade as that user.

probes.description: Replay captured session cookie on WS upgrade after the underlying HTTP session has logged out. If 101 returned, session-bound auth is broken on WS path.

ws_origin_strip.description: WebSocket upgrade with Origin header REMOVED (vs evil.com). Some servers default-allow when Origin is missing, treating the request as same-origin/non-browser. Browsers always send Origin on WS upgrades.

probes.description: WS upgrade WITHOUT Origin header. If 101 returned, attacker non-browser client (curl, Python websockets) gains full access — useful for credential stuffing or rate-limit bypass.

subprotocol_confusion.description: WS subprotocol confusion — server accepts unrecognized Sec-WebSocket-Protocol value and switches to a less-validated path.

probes.description: Upgrade with Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: admin / debug / internal. Some implementations have hidden subprotocols with reduced auth.

ws_frame_smuggling.description: WebSocket frame smuggling — server reassembles WebSocket frames differently from intermediate proxy. Combined with HTTP/1.1 smuggling, can hijack connection.

probes.description: [Manual] Send fragmented WS frames with conflicting FIN bits. If proxy and origin disagree, frame-smuggling primitive established. Test with browser_navigate or burp_websocket_send.

permessage_deflate_compression_oracle.description: WebSocket per-message-deflate compression (RFC 7692) leaks plaintext via compressed-size deltas — a CRIME-class side channel. If attacker can submit chosen plaintext that gets concatenated with secret tokens (e.g. echo-server, debug-broadcast), they recover token bytes one at a time.

probes.description: Detect per-message-deflate via Sec-WebSocket-Extensions response header. Presence = compression oracle prerequisite met.

[Manual] If echo-server with chosen-plaintext primitive: send messages with prefix matching guess of token (`token=0`, `token=1`, ...). Compressed frame size drops by 1+ byte when prefix matches actual token bytes (deflate back-reference). Recover token byte-by-byte.

Strip Sec-WebSocket-Extensions: permessage-deflate from handshake — if server insists on compressing anyway (config-forced), oracle persists for ALL clients. Most servers downgrade gracefully; check both behaviors.

per_message_authz_drift_2025.description: WebSocket per-message BOLA — connection auth'd as user A but each frame carries a target identifier (target_user_id / chat_id / room_id) that is NOT re-checked against the session identity. Distinct from cswsh (handshake) and auth_bypass (no auth at all): the handshake IS authenticated, the drift is in per-frame authz. Common in chat / collaboration / notification servers that scale frame-handlers stateless. 2025 wave of bug-bounty findings on Slack / Discord-clone / collab tools.

probes.description: Per-frame target swap — send legit-shape frame with victim's ID in the target field while the session belongs to attacker. Server should reject (frame-level authz); accepting + returning victim data confirms per-message BOLA.
Subscribe to victim-scoped channel from attacker's session. Server must verify channel ownership; broadcasting victim's notifications to attacker confirms per-channel authz miss.

marimo_websocket_terminal_rce_2026.description: CVE-2026-39987 — Marimo Python notebook ≤0.20.4 exposes a Terminal WebSocket endpoint without authentication. Unauth attacker opens the WS, sends shell commands, gets PTY output. Class: AI/ML notebook → host RCE. Increasingly common as Marimo (Jupyter-alternative for reactive notebooks) sees enterprise adoption; instances exposed to LAN or accidentally to internet.

probes.description: Open the Marimo terminal WebSocket (default path /terminal/ws or /api/terminal). Vulnerable instance accepts without auth; send `id\n` and expect uid= shell output. Use websocket_connect + websocket_send_message via Praetor's WS tools.
